claims.claim: Binocular rivalry normally yields alternating conscious dominance while neural activity tracks the currently reported percept.

source.title: Neural correlates of binocular rivalry in the human lateral geniculate nucleus

counter_sources.title: Scale-freeness of dominant and piecemeal perceptions during binocular rivalry
counter_sources.relation: qualifies winner-take-all wording by measuring mixed piecemeal percepts

claims.shows: Competing visual inputs and percept-linked gating can be measured at multiple processing stages.
claims.does_not_show: It does not show two simultaneous conscious percepts, a quantum wavefunction, or physical collapse.

claims.claim: Predictive-coding models can retain residual evidence for a suppressed interpretation during bistable perception.

source.title: A predictive coding account of bistable perception - a model-based fMRI study

claims.shows: A classical probabilistic model can represent competing hypotheses and residual prediction error.
claims.does_not_show: Residual evidence is not evidence that both alternatives are consciously experienced at once.

claims.claim: For the neural degrees of freedom modeled by Tegmark, estimated decoherence is far faster than neural computation.

source.title: Importance of quantum decoherence in brain processes

counter_sources.title: Quantum computation in brain microtubules: decoherence and biological feasibility
counter_sources.relation: disputes modeled substrate and parameters without supplying cognitive evidence

claims.shows: A quantitative physical objection must specify the degrees of freedom and compare decoherence with neural timescales.
claims.does_not_show: It does not prove that every possible quantum-biological contribution is impossible; its modeled assumptions have been disputed.

claims.claim: Tip-of-the-tongue states recruit retrieval and metacognitive monitoring systems.

source.title: Tip-of-the-tongue and the retrieval of known words: an event-related fMRI study

claims.shows: Knowing that retrieval is failing is neurally and behaviorally distinguishable from successful recall.
claims.does_not_show: It does not identify candidate words as a literal superposition awaiting collapse.

claims.claim: Loss of consciousness under several anesthetics covaries with reduced complexity of cortical responses, while ketamine can preserve complex responses and dreams.

source.title: Consciousness and Complexity during Unresponsiveness Induced by Propofol, Xenon, and Ketamine

claims.shows: Conscious state is better separated by effective complexity than by behavioral responsiveness alone.
claims.does_not_show: It does not locate a detector or a stopped wavefunction-collapse process.

claims.claim: The default mode is an organized intrinsic baseline, not a state in which the brain is inactive or has no thought.

source.title: A default mode of brain function

claims.shows: Resting and task-negative activity has stable organization and a measurable metabolic baseline.
claims.does_not_show: Default-mode activity is not evidence for quantum coherence or a privileged wave mode.

claims.claim: Task-positive and default-mode networks can exhibit anticorrelated intrinsic dynamics.

source.title: The human brain is intrinsically organized into dynamic, anticorrelated functional networks

counter_sources.title: The impact of global signal regression on resting state correlations: are anti-correlated networks introduced?
counter_sources.relation: shows that preprocessing can introduce or amplify anticorrelation

claims.shows: Large-scale networks can alternate or oppose one another without an external task.
claims.does_not_show: Anticorrelation does not establish wave versus detector modes or an observer switch.

claims.claim: A preregistered adversarial comparison found results that fit parts of IIT and GNWT while challenging key predictions of both.

source.title: Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness

claims.shows: Consciousness theories can be compared through preregistered divergent predictions rather than analogical resemblance.
claims.does_not_show: Neither IIT nor GNWT supplies evidence for quantum coherence or transformer consciousness.

claims.claim: Many attention heads can be removed after training with little task-performance loss, and some layers can be reduced to one head.

source.title: Are Sixteen Heads Really Better than One?

claims.shows: Head count is not a direct monotonic measure of independent interpretations or depth.
claims.does_not_show: Prunability does not mean heads never specialize or interact.

claims.claim: A minority of specialized attention heads can carry much of the measured task contribution.

source.title: Analyzing Multi-Head Self-Attention: Specialized Heads Do the Heavy Lifting, the Rest Can Be Pruned

claims.shows: Different heads may implement different functions, but contribution is sparse and task-dependent.
claims.does_not_show: A head is not a slit, observer, semantic interpretation, or conscious perspective.

claims.claim: Neural-network superposition can mean packing more sparse features than available representation dimensions.
claims.shows: Feature interference and polysemanticity can arise in classical sparse representation learning.
claims.does_not_show: The word superposition here does not imply complex amplitudes, phase, Born probabilities, entanglement, or consciousness.

claims.claim: Intermediate transformer states can be probed as evolving token predictions with a learned lens.

source.title: Eliciting Latent Predictions from Transformers with the Tuned Lens

claims.shows: Layerwise prediction trajectories are an operational alternative to calling the residual stream a 5D probability manifold.
claims.does_not_show: A probe-defined distribution is not an intrinsic quantum state and can introduce probe artifacts.

claims.claim: Attention weights alone are not a reliable explanation of a model output.

counter_sources.title: Attention is not not Explanation
counter_sources.relation: argues that explanatory value depends on the test and supplies alternative diagnostics

claims.shows: Different attention distributions can yield equivalent predictions and can disagree with gradient importance.
claims.does_not_show: It does not prove attention is never informative; later work showed the answer depends on the explanatory test.

claims.claim: Semantic entropy can group multiple generated strings by meaning before measuring uncertainty.

source.title: Detecting hallucinations in large language models using semantic entropy

claims.shows: Meaning-level diversity is measurable and is more relevant than raw token or string entropy for some tasks.
claims.does_not_show: High semantic entropy is not automatically depth, truth, consciousness, or useful oscillation.

claims.claim: LLMs can exhibit lingering interpretations and reanalysis on garden-path sentences.

source.title: Incremental Comprehension of Garden-Path Sentences by Large Language Models

claims.shows: Temporary ambiguity and later reinterpretation can be tested behaviorally in language models.
claims.does_not_show: Alignment with human error patterns is not evidence of shared phenomenology or physical superposition.

claims.claim: Multiple meanings of an ambiguous word can be activated briefly before context selects among them.

source.title: Lexical access during sentence comprehension: (Re)consideration of context effects

claims.shows: Human language processing can transiently activate rival lexical meanings before selection.
claims.does_not_show: Parallel lexical activation is a classical processing result, not quantum superposition or enduring non-collapse.

claims.claim: Semantic ambiguity increases activity in parts of the frontotemporal language network.

source.title: The Neural Mechanisms of Speech Comprehension: fMRI studies of Semantic Ambiguity

claims.shows: Ambiguity resolution has measurable processing costs and neural correlates.
claims.does_not_show: More activation does not mean more depth, consciousness, or interference in the quantum sense.

claims.claim: Repetitive speech can produce widespread cortical deactivation relative to controls.

source.title: Repetitive speech elicits widespread deactivation in the human cortex: the Mantra effect?

claims.shows: Repetition can alter measured brain state even without assigning special semantics to the repeated phrase.
claims.does_not_show: It does not show preserved semantic superposition, a language without observer, or a special quantum mechanism.

claims.claim: Apparent role separation in language models can be carried by shallow lexical shortcuts rather than a deep role representation.

source.title: The Illusion of Role Separation: Hidden Shortcuts in LLM Role Learning (and How to Fix Them)

claims.shows: Role-conditioned behavior needs controls for wording shortcuts and generalization across paraphrases.
claims.does_not_show: It does not establish that every role effect is shallow or that relationship history has no effect.
